summary: 9 items surfaced from 26 qualifying stories, with strongest hits in dev tools, apps, and hardware.

items.name: Better Auth
items.source_story_title: From Supabase to Clerk to Better Auth
items.summary: TypeScript auth framework that keeps users in your own database.
items.why_included: HN commenters framed it as a lower-risk alternative to auth SaaS like Clerk, Supabase, and Auth0.
items.evidence: Discussion repeatedly praised the own-database approach and warned that outsourcing auth adds reliability and lock-in risk.

items.name: Mise
items.source_story_title: Going Full Time on Open Source
items.summary: Lightweight dev-environment and task runner for polyglot projects.
items.why_included: Commenters recommended it as the simpler default before reaching for Nix or Bazel.
items.evidence: The thread praised its caching, monorepo fit, and the maintainer going full time as good news for the project.

items.name: Templatical
items.source_story_title: Show HN: I built an open-source email builder, alternative to Beefree/Unlayer
items.summary: Self-hosted email builder that outputs MJML from a Vue editor.
items.why_included: HN liked the open-source, client-side approach and the alternative to commercial email builders.
items.evidence: Comments highlighted Vue 3 + TipTap, zero telemetry, custom blocks, and keeping sending with the user's own provider.

items.name: FreshRSS
items.source_story_title: YouTube, your RSS feeds are broken
items.summary: Self-hosted RSS reader used to filter broken YouTube feeds.
items.why_included: Commenters shared it as a practical workaround for feed cleanup and Shorts filtering.
items.evidence: The discussion said FreshRSS can mark Shorts-related URLs read and help salvage YouTube subscriptions.

items.name: Inkscape 1.4.4
items.summary: Open-source vector editor for SVG cleanup, PDF extraction, and bitmap tracing.
items.why_included: HN commenters described it as a mature, practical design tool despite a few SVG quirks.
items.evidence: People praised its usefulness for extracting design elements from PDFs and vectorizing bitmaps while noting formatting and color limitations.

items.name: Proton Meet
items.summary: End-to-end encrypted video conferencing from Proton.
items.why_included: The thread included real usage reports, not just product-positioning debate.
items.evidence: Commenters said it works well in practice while others argued about Proton's product strategy and missing features.

items.name: Steam Controller CAD files
items.source_story_title: Valve releases Steam Controller CAD files under Creative Commons license
items.summary: Valve's Creative Commons shell models for the Steam Controller and puck.
items.why_included: Commenters were excited about open hardware files for modding, accessories, and replacement parts.
items.evidence: Discussion centered on 3D-printed accessories, accessibility tweaks, and the practical value of the released CAD.

items.name: CARA 2.0
items.source_story_title: CARA 2.0 – “I Built a Better Robot Dog”
items.summary: Hobbyist quadruped robot dog built from cheap drone motors.
items.why_included: HN discussion focused on the impressive hardware stack and the cost/performance tradeoffs.
items.evidence: Commenters praised the motor/controller choices, the documentation, and the engineering limits around heat and budget.

items.name: Micron 6600 ION Data Center SSD
items.source_story_title: 245TB Micron 6600 ION Data Center SSD Now Shipping
items.summary: 245TB enterprise SSD pushing extreme storage density.
items.why_included: The thread was all about the density, price, and enterprise use case.
items.evidence: Commenters debated QLC NAND, write speed, rack density, and whether the economics make sense for datacenters.
